penalties: Interop risk: banner: Minor: some AT don't announce banner landmark by default

evidence.description: Target role, name, and structural position came from the browser accessibility snapshot.
Best path and navigation cost were computed from captured targets and AT profile action weights.
Scores, severity, action type, penalties, and fixes were derived from Tactual scoring heuristics.

penalties: Target is 56×17px, below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum (24×24 for AA). Users with motor impairments have a higher miss rate on small targets. Screen-reader users aren't affected, but touch/pointer users are.
suggestedFixes: Increase min-width and min-height to 24px, or enlarge the clickable area via padding. An invisible padded hit region can enlarge touch target without changing visual design.

penalties: Target is not efficiently reachable via heading or landmark navigation

Target is 40×17px, below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum (24×24 for AA). Users with motor impairments have a higher miss rate on small targets. Screen-reader users aren't affected, but touch/pointer users are.
suggestedFixes: Add a heading or landmark near this target to enable skip navigation
Increase min-width and min-height to 24px, or enlarge the clickable area via padding. An invisible padded hit region can enlarge touch target without changing visual design.

diagnostics.message: No skip-to-content link found. Keyboard and screen-reader users must Tab through all navigation elements to reach the main content. A skip link is the single most impactful fix for navigation cost. Concrete savings: a skip link would bypass 3 controls (38% of page interactive targets).
Structural overview: 5 headings · landmarks: main, banner, contentinfo, navigation · skip link: no · 19 total targets

4 text elements lose contrast under simulated color-vision deficiency (tritanopia: 4). Examples (tritanopia): a "Products" — 9.4:1 normal → 2.44:1 tritanopia (needs 4.5:1); a "About" — 9.4:1 normal → 2.44:1 tritanopia (needs 4.5:1); a "Contact" — 9.4:1 normal → 2.44:1 tritanopia (needs 4.5:1). Approximately 8% of male and 0.5% of female users have a CVD; choose color combinations whose contrast survives the deuteranopia/protanopia/tritanopia transforms or rely on luminance + non-color cues.
